issues.description: FR-5b: The round query parameter is accepted but ignored (`let _ = query.round;`). The spec says the diff endpoint should scope to a round's commit range when the round param is provided. Per-round diffing is silently a no-op, always returning the full branch diff.
issues.suggestion: Either implement round-scoped diffing by looking up commit SHAs from the round records, or return an error when round param is provided to signal the limitation rather than silently ignoring it.

issues.description: FR-3d: Cancel confirmation has no 3-second timeout. The spec says 'y within 3s', but `cancel_confirm_pending` persists indefinitely until the user presses any key. If user presses 'x' then walks away and returns 5 minutes later, the next keypress still activates the confirmation flow.
issues.suggestion: Store the instant when cancel_confirm_pending was set. In the input handler, check if more than 3 seconds have elapsed and auto-clear the flag if so.

issues.description: spawn_batch_inspect_task re-fetches status for ALL loops every 5s with sequential HTTP requests. For N loops, this makes N+1 serial requests (1 status + N inspect). This duplicates the existing 2s status poller and could take many seconds for large teams, causing stale header data.
issues.suggestion: Share the loop list from the main status poller via a watch channel instead of re-fetching. Parallelize inspect fetches with futures::join_all to reduce latency.

issues.description: calculate_loop_round_cost applies a single model's pricing to ALL token usage across ALL stages in a round. In reality, the implementor model (e.g. Opus at $15/M input) is used for implement/test stages, while the reviewer model (e.g. Haiku at $1/M input) is used for review/audit. Using only the implementor pricing for all tokens will significantly overestimate cost when the reviewer is a cheaper model.
issues.suggestion: Split token usage by stage and apply implementor pricing to implement/test tokens and reviewer pricing to review/audit tokens.

issues.description: FR-4a: Writing bell character to stdout via crossterm::execute! during the event processing loop while ratatui owns the terminal. While crossterm's execute! is safer than raw stdout write, it bypasses ratatui's rendering pipeline and can cause display artifacts on some terminals.
issues.suggestion: Queue the bell and emit it during the next terminal.draw() call, or use crossterm's queue! macro and flush during the render cycle.

issues.description: FR-4a: The spec says convergence notification should include PR URL in the format 'CONVERGED: agent/dev/foo → https://...'. The implementation only includes the PR URL in the flash when pr_url is Some, but the non-PR branch (`None => format!("{spec_name} → {new_state}")`) omits the checkmark prefix that the Some branch has (`✓ {new_state}: ...`), creating inconsistent formatting.
issues.suggestion: Use consistent formatting for both branches, e.g., always prefix with the state transition indicator.

issues.description: FR-1c: The --team header shows 'team view' but doesn't break down by engineer as the spec says it should 'label accordingly'. This is a minor spec interpretation gap but worth noting.
issues.suggestion: Consider grouping or labeling loop counts by engineer in team view header.

issues.description: Log lines are cloned for both the selected loop's log buffer and the multi_logs map. For high-volume streams this doubles memory allocation per line.
issues.suggestion: Use Arc<String> or Rc<String> to share ownership between both buffers.

issues.description: extract_verdict returns theme colors, but the function signature takes &Theme which is good. However, the theme colors are only used for the non-selected row case - selected rows override fg to theme.text, which means verdict colors are invisible for the selected row.
issues.suggestion: Consider keeping verdict color visible even for the selected row, perhaps in a different element or via background color variation.

issues.description: highlight_style uses theme.surface as background, which is the same as the block background of the loop selector. This means the selected row highlight may be invisible or very subtle.
issues.suggestion: Use a distinct highlight background color, possibly by adding a highlight_bg field to Theme or deriving one from the surface color.
